input.background: Exercises 16.2 and 16.4 considered a problem in which seven of eight patients expressed a preference for medical procedure A compared to medical procedure B. A Normal approximation test was precluded because of the small sample size.
input.question: Test the hypothesis of equal preference for medical procedure A and medical procedure B with Fisher's procedure.

output.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: p=0.50$ versus $H_2: p>0.50$ B. Test statistic. An exact binomial test is used because of the small sample size. Under $H_0, X \sim \mathrm{~b}(8,0.5)$. The observed number of success in the sample is $x=$ 7. C. $\boldsymbol{P}$-value. $P$ (one-sided) $=\operatorname{Pr}(X=7)+\operatorname{Pr}(X=8)=0.0313+0.0039=$ 0.0352 . This provided good evidence against the null hypothesis. D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against $H_0$ is significant at $\alpha=0.05$ (reject $H_0$ ) but is not significant at $\alpha=0.01$ (retain $H_0$ ).

Patient_Preference.description: The preference expressed by each patient for medical procedure A or B.

Total_Patients.description: The total number of patients in the study.

Procedure_A_Count.description: The number of patients who expressed a preference for medical procedure A.

Procedure_B_Count.description: The number of patients who expressed a preference for medical procedure B.
